title: When Scientists Collaborate with Artists
subtitle: 科学家与艺术家联手：跨界如何催生新发现

paragraphs.en: Enlightening, challenging, stimulating, fun. These were some of the words that Nature readers used to describe their experience of art-science collaborations in a series of articles on partnerships between artists and researchers. Nearly 40% of the roughly 350 people who responded to an accompanying poll said they had collaborated with artists; and almost all said they would consider doing so in future. Such an encouraging result is not surprising.
paragraphs.zh: 启迪人心、充满挑战、激发灵感、趣味盎然——这些都是《自然》杂志的读者在一系列关于艺术家与研究者合作的文章中，用来描述自身艺术与科学合作体验的词语。在参与附带民意调查的约350人中，近40%表示曾与艺术家合作过，且几乎所有人都称未来会考虑这样做。这一结果令人鼓舞，却并不令人意外。

words.meaning: adj. 激发灵感的；刺激的

paragraphs.en: Scientists are increasingly seeking out visual artists to help them to communicate their work to new audiences. “Artists help scientists reach a broader audience and make emotional connections that enhance learning,” one respondent said. One example of how artists and scientists have together rocked the senses came last month when the Sydney Symphony Orchestra performed a reworked version of Antonio Vivaldi's The Four Seasons.
paragraphs.zh: 科学家正越来越多地寻求与视觉艺术家合作，以帮助自己将研究成果传达给新的受众。一位受访者表示：“艺术家能帮助科学家触及更广泛的受众，并建立情感联结，从而增强学习效果。”上个月，悉尼交响乐团演奏了安东尼奥·维瓦尔第《四季》的改编版，这便是艺术家与科学家携手震撼感官的一个例证。

paragraphs.en: They reimagined the 300-year-old score by injecting the latest climate prediction data for each season — provided by Monash University's Climate Change Communication Research Hub. The performance was a creative call to action ahead of November's United Nations Climate Change Conference in Glasgow, UK. But a genuine partnership must be a two-way street.
paragraphs.zh: 他们为这份有着三百年历史的乐谱注入了由莫纳什大学气候变化传播研究中心提供的每个季节的最新气候预测数据，从而重新构想了这部作品。这场演出是在11月英国格拉斯哥联合国气候变化大会召开之前，发出的一次富有创意的行动呼吁。然而，真正的合作关系必须是双向的。

words.meaning: n. 有来有往的关系；双向过程

paragraphs.en: Fewer artists than scientists responded to the Nature poll; however, several respondents noted that artists do not simply assist scientists with their communication requirements. Nor should their work be considered only as an object of study. The alliances are most valuable when scientists and artists have a shared stake in a project, are able to jointly design it and can critique each other's work.
paragraphs.zh: 参与《自然》杂志调查的艺术家少于科学家，但若干受访者指出，艺术家并非只是协助科学家满足其传播需求。艺术家的作品也不应仅仅被视为研究对象。当科学家与艺术家在项目中有共同利益、能够共同设计项目并相互批评对方的作品时，这种联盟才最具价值。

paragraphs.en: Such an approach can both prompt new research as well as result in powerful art. More than half a century ago, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology opened its Center for Advanced Visual Studies to explore the role of technology in culture. The founders deliberately focused their projects around light — hence the “visual studies” in the name.
paragraphs.zh: 这种做法既能催生新的研究，也能造就极具力量的艺术作品。半个多世纪前，麻省理工学院成立了高级视觉研究中心，旨在探索技术在文化中的作用。中心的创立者们特意将项目聚焦于“光”这一主题，名称中的“视觉研究”即由此而来。

paragraphs.en: Light was something that both artists and scientists had an interest in, and therefore could form the basis of collaboration. As science and technology progressed, and divided into more sub-disciplines, the centre was simultaneously looking to a time when leading researchers could also be artists, writers and poets, and vice versa. Nature's poll findings suggest that this trend is as strong as ever.
paragraphs.zh: 光既是艺术家感兴趣的对象，也为科学家所关注，因而能够成为合作的基础。随着科学技术的进步及其分支学科的不断细化，该中心同时也期盼着一个时代：一流的研究者可以同时是艺术家、作家和诗人，反之亦然。《自然》杂志的民意调查结果表明，这一趋势至今依然强劲。

paragraphs.en: But to make a collaboration work, both sides need to invest time and embrace surprise and challenge. The reach of art-science tie-ups needs to go beyond the necessary purpose of research communication, and participants must not fall into the trap of stereotyping each other. Artists and scientists alike are immersed in discovery and invention, and challenge and critique are core to both, too.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，要让合作真正奏效，双方都需要投入时间，并乐于拥抱意外与挑战。艺术与科学的结合不应局限于研究传播这一必要目的，参与者还须避免落入彼此刻板印象的陷阱。艺术家与科学家同样沉浸于发现与发明之中，而挑战与批判也是两者共同的核心特质。
